message:
perf(connection): enable single-connection cache fast-path on schema-only read-only opens (all VFS variants)

`Connection::open_schema_only(path)` opens a read-only pager backed by
the platform-appropriate VFS (IoUringVfs on Linux, UnixVfs elsewhere
on Unix, WindowsVfs on Windows). Schema-only opens are by definition
single-connection, single-reader workloads — the calling site has
exclusive access for the lifetime of the returned Connection.

The pager carries a single-connection-cache fast-path that bypasses
the multi-connection coordination overhead (no shared-cache lookups,
no inter-connection invalidation tracking, no atomic load fences for
cross-connection page-version checks). Previously it was opt-in via
the SimplePager builder API and `open_schema_only` left it disabled,
so schema-only reads paid the multi-connection overhead unnecessarily.

This commit calls `pager.enable_single_connection_cache_fast_path()`
on the freshly-built pager in all three platform branches (IoUring /
Unix / Windows). The call returns Result, but failure is non-fatal
(the pager continues to work with the safe multi-connection path),
so the return value is `let _ =`-discarded.

New test coverage:

  test_open_schema_only_enables_single_connection_cache_fast_path_on_iouring
    (Linux): opens the same DB twice, schema-only on the second open,
    asserts pager.cache.is_fast_path_enabled() returns true.

  test_open_schema_only_enables_single_connection_cache_fast_path_on_unix
    (Unix non-Linux): same shape against the UnixVfs branch.

Windows currently lacks an equivalent test because the test harness
doesn't run a Windows job in CI; the runtime call still happens via
the cfg(target_os = "windows") branch.

Effect: schema-only opens (commonly used for migrations, schema
introspection, and ANALYZE-style read-only workloads) now skip the
multi-connection coordination cost they were never going to need
in their actual use pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
